# Build Packet — 8d: Item / Product master (bidirectional onboarding → ERPNext-mastered)

**This is an approved, frozen build packet. It is your input, not a working file. Do not edit this file or SPEC.md. If anything here is wrong, incomplete, or contradicts the committed code, STOP and report it — do not silently reconcile or build around it (CLAUDE.md §0).**

## What this is, and a warning about its size

8d is the **largest** master — a bidirectional item sync with a two-phase model (supervised bidirectional onboarding, then ERPNext-mastered steady state). Read §8.1 (appended in full) before anything else; it is the authoritative model and this packet implements it exactly.

**Build it in STAGES, in the order below, testing each stage green before starting the next.** Do NOT attempt the whole bidirectional engine in one shot — it will be untestable. The stages are dependency-ordered: the pull is read-only toward EasyEcom and proves the substrate; the push mutates EasyEcom and must come after. Commit at the end of each stage (locally; the user reviews/pushes).

This packet depends on: the Field Mapping engine (§5/§8.0), 8c's tax resolver (`resolve_and_stamp_tax`), the 8a per-record savepoint helper (`_isolation.py`), and the workflow-fixture pattern (+ its 5 captured gotchas, incl. `safe_eval`/`len()`).

## The five EasyEcom Product Master API functions (all grounded in real payloads + the API doc)

- `GET /Products/GetProductMaster` — the pull. Params: `includeLocations` (1 = account-wide, USE THIS), `updated_after` (incremental), `active`, `product_type`, `category`, `cpIds`, `limit` (max 200), `custom_fields`. Cursor pagination via `nextUrl`. Headers: `x-api-key` + `Bearer <jwt>`.
- `GET /Products/GetProductMastersCount` — `{count: N}`; for progress/paging math.
- `POST /Products/CreateMasterProduct` — push-create; returns `{data: {product_id}}`. Mandatory: Brand, Sku, Category, ModelNumber, Cost, Weight, Length, Height, Width, TaxRate, materialType. itemType (Child=0/Combo=1/Kit=2). subProducts (≥2, only itemType 1/2).
- `POST /Products/UpdateMasterProduct` — push-update; keys on sku OR productId; partial updates OK.
- `POST /Products/ActivateDeactivateProduct` — keys on product_id; status 1/0.

**Doc-vs-payload divergences already identified (handle the PAYLOAD reality, not the doc's):**
- `product_type` is a STRING in the response (`normal_product`/`variant_parent`/`child_product`/`combo_product`) but the doc's request-filter and create-side enums are INTEGERS and inconsistent with each other. Branch on the **response string**.
- `materialType` is mandatory on create but ABSENT from the response — must be manufactured on push (default Finished Good=1).
- `accounting_unit` is the UOM signal but is dirty in real data ("111", "PCS") — needs a default-UOM transform.
- `EANUPC` can be garbage ("testUomcode001") — not used for matching (we match sku==item_code only).
- `cp_inventory` is explicitly ignorable. `product_id`/`cp_id` are internal (store for push, don't use as join key — sku is the key).

## STAGE 1 — The substrate: EasyEcom Item Map + mode flag

Build first, on its own, tested before any sync wiring:
- **`EasyEcom Item Map` DocType** (§8.1.2): `ee_sku` (unique, the join key), a link to the ERPNext **Item OR Product Bundle** (Dynamic Link, or a `link_type` + `link_name` pair — your call, but it MUST accommodate both object types), `ee_product_id`, `ee_cp_id` (stored for push), and a status field with values: Mapped, Created-Flagged, Flagged-Not-Created, Drift, Disabled. DB UNIQUE on `ee_sku`.
- **`item_master_mode`** setting (`onboarding` | `erpnext_mastered`) on the EasyEcom Account (or per-Company settings — match where the other account-level config lives). Default `onboarding`. An explicit FDE action flips it (a button/field with a confirmation — this is the "onboarding complete, ERPNext is now master" gate).
- Tests: map row CRUD, the dual-object link works for both an Item and a Product Bundle, UNIQUE on ee_sku enforced, mode flag defaults to onboarding and flips.

## STAGE 2 — EE → ERPNext pull (the inbound onboarding flow)

- **Reconcile the `EasyEcom-Item-Pull` engine ruleset against the real `GetProductMaster` payload FIRST** (the shipped §5 ruleset is stale — written from an assumed payload; it has `item_code` where EE sends `sku`, etc. — same stale-ruleset situation as Location-Pull and Channel-Pull). Run the real payload through Test Mapping; fix every path. Map: sku, product_name, description, brand, colour, size, dimensions (units: EE weight=g, dims=cm — handle ERPNext's expected units), mrp, cost, hsn_code→gst_hsn_code, accounting_unit→stock_uom (**default-UOM transform** for dirty/missing), active→disabled (transform), custom_fields. Express derivations as ruleset transforms (§8.0 — engine = API-change insurance).
- **The pull job** (§8.1.4): cursor-paginated, count-aware, **resumable background job** — read GetProductMastersCount, walk `nextUrl` page-by-page (≤200/page), **savepoint-isolated per product** (reuse 8a `_isolation.py`), resume from last cursor if interrupted. NOT a single synchronous call (it would time out on ~15k products). `includeLocations=1` (account-wide). Full pull on onboarding; `updated_after` deltas on schedule.
- **Matching** (§8.1.3): map row exists → use it; else exact `sku == item_code` (byte-for-byte) → auto-map + create map row; else → create new Item + new map row. No fuzzy/EAN matching, no matching-review queue.
- **product_type branching** (on the response STRING): `normal_product` → Item; `combo_product` → Product Bundle (Stage 4 — for now flag/skip or stub); `variant_parent`/`child_product`/`kit_bom`/**unknown** → **flag Flagged-Not-Created, do NOT create.**
- **Tax**: call 8c's `resolve_and_stamp_tax(item, product)` per item.
- **Content gating** (§8.1.4): a creatable product missing HSN / with unmapped tax / dirty UOM → **create + flag Created-Flagged** (the SKU must exist for downstream orders; never hold, never silent-default).
- `active: 0` → disable the item.
- Tests: cursor walk + resume; savepoint isolation (one bad product doesn't abort the page); count-aware paging; exact-match auto-map vs create-new; product_type branching incl. flag-not-created for variant/child/kit/unknown; content-gating create+flag; tax-resolver integration; active→disabled; ruleset reconciliation against the real payload fixture.

## STAGE 3 — ERPNext → EE push (outbound onboarding + the steady-state mechanism)

**This stage MUTATES EasyEcom (CreateMasterProduct actually creates products in EE). Smoke-test with a disposable test product and clean up — unlike the read-only pull.**
- ERPNext item → EE create-payload translation (§8.1.5). **Manufacture EE-mandatory fields ERPNext lacks:** materialType → default **Finished Good=1**; itemType (normal=0); Brand/Category/ModelNumber (source from item or default — flag if a hard-mandatory field can't be sourced). ProductTaxCode = item HSN. TaxRate from item tax (EE allowed: 0/3/5/12/18/28). Dimensions in EE units.
- CreateMasterProduct (new) / UpdateMasterProduct (changed; keys sku or productId; partial) / on create, write returned `product_id` to the map row.
- Tests: create-payload field manufacturing (materialType default, itemType, etc.); product_id written back to map; update partial; a missing-mandatory-field case → flagged not pushed. (Mutating sandbox tests: gated/marked so they don't run against prod; use a disposable SKU.)

## STAGE 4 — Product Bundle ↔ EasyEcom combo (bidirectional)

- **Pull** (complete what Stage 2 stubbed): EE `combo_product` → ERPNext **Product Bundle**; its `sub_products` → bundle components, **each resolved to an ERPNext item via its own map row** (the map is the component-identity resolver).
- **Push**: ERPNext **Product Bundle** → EE **combo** ("virtual combo" — non-stocked wrapper; components carry stock). `CreateMasterProduct` with `itemType=1` + `subProducts` array, **each component resolved to its EE sku via its own map row**.
- **Dependency ordering**: components must exist in EE (and be mapped) before the combo is pushed — process **components before combos**; missing/unmapped component at combo-push → **flag, do NOT push** (no broken combos).
- **Constraint**: EE combo requires **≥2 subProducts**; a smaller bundle → flag, can't push.
- The bundle gets its **own map row** (bundle ↔ combo); components resolve via their rows.
- **No BOM / no Kit**: ERPNext BOMs are NOT pushed; EE `kit_bom` is flagged-not-created on pull. Only Bundles ↔ combos cross.
- Tests: pull combo→bundle with component resolution; push bundle→combo with component resolution + dependency ordering; missing-component → flag-not-push; <2-component → flag; bundle map row + dual-object link; BOM never pushed.

## STAGE 5 — Lifecycle + post-flip drift detection

- **Lifecycle** (§8.1.7): onboarding — EE active flag → ERPNext disable/enable (pull), ERPNext disable → EE ActivateDeactivateProduct (push). Steady state — ERPNext disable/enable → push to EE. Never delete.
- **The flip + drift** (§8.1.8): after `item_master_mode = erpnext_mastered`, the push is authoritative; the pull becomes **drift detection only** — an EE-origin new product or an EE-side edit to a mapped item is **flagged Drift for the FDE**, NOT accepted as truth and NOT auto-overwritten. The FDE decides per drift flag.
- **Supervised reconciliation** (§8.1.9): during onboarding, duplicates/conflicts surfaced; FDE resolves; then flips.
- Tests: deactivate/reactivate both directions phase-governed; post-flip EE-origin product → Drift flag (not created/accepted); post-flip EE edit to mapped item → Drift flag (not overwritten); the flip changes pull behaviour from accept to drift-detect.

## Deferred / out of scope (do NOT build)
- **Marketplace listings** (Amazon ASIN, Flipkart FSN, per-channel marketplace_sku) — separate per-channel listing sync (§8.1.11).
- **Stock / inventory sync** (per-location quantities) — separate concern from the product catalogue.
- No item duplication per Company (use ERPNext Item Defaults — standard mechanism).

## Principles this packet must honor
- **§8.0 engine policy** — payload→field translation via the `EasyEcom-Item-Pull` ruleset (reconciled to real payload), never hardcoded. Flow code orchestrates (cursor job, branching, matching, tax call, gating, push).
- **§2.7 no silent failure** — every exception (content problem, unsupported type, drift, missing component, unpushable) is a VISIBLE flagged state, never a silent default or silent overwrite.
- **§7.1 isolation** — the pull batch reuses the 8a savepoint helper; one product's failure never aborts the page/walk.
- **Frappe-native** — ERPNext Item, Product Bundle, Item Defaults, standard Workflow; the map is a standard DocType.
- **Identity is never auto-guessed** — match only on exact sku==item_code; everything else creates new (a visible duplicate) rather than risk a wrong link (silent corruption). Remap/divergence is FDE-resolved, never automatic.

---

# Build instruction

Build in the five stages above, **in order, each green before the next**. Commit locally at each stage boundary (the user reviews/pushes; do NOT push). 

1. **Inventory first**: confirm ERPNext Item + Product Bundle DocTypes and their fields; the `EasyEcom-Item-Pull` ruleset's current (stale) state; 8c's `resolve_and_stamp_tax` signature; the 8a `_isolation.py` helper; the workflow-fixture pattern. Report drift; if anything contradicts §8.1, STOP and report.
2. **Stage 1** (map + mode substrate) → test → commit.
3. **Stage 2** (pull: reconcile ruleset first, then the cursor job, matching, branching, tax, gating) → test → commit. This is the big one; the pull is read-only toward EE so it's safe to exercise against the sandbox.
4. **Stage 3** (push) → test → commit. MUTATING — use a disposable test SKU, clean up, gate mutating tests.
5. **Stage 4** (bundle ↔ combo both ways, component resolution, dependency ordering) → test → commit.
6. **Stage 5** (lifecycle + flip + drift) → test → commit.

**Done when:** bench migrate clean; the map links items/bundles both ways; the pull ingests the real catalogue (cursor-walked, resumable, savepoint-isolated) with correct branching/matching/tax/gating; the push creates EE products/combos with manufactured mandatory fields and writes back product_id; bundles round-trip as virtual combos with component resolution + dependency ordering; lifecycle works both ways; the flip switches the pull to drift-detection; suite green.

**Report at each stage**: what you verified vs built, the ruleset reconciliation (Stage 2), the cursor/resume + savepoint behaviour, the matching outcomes, the push field-manufacturing + product_id writeback, the bundle component-resolution + dependency ordering, and the flip/drift behaviour. For the mutating push smoke (Stage 3), report exactly what was created in the sandbox and that it was cleaned up.

---

# Spec body — §8.1 Item / Product master (authoritative)

## 8.1 Item / Product master (bidirectional onboarding → ERPNext-mastered steady state)

Item sync is the largest master. It is **bidirectional during onboarding** and **ERPNext-mastered in steady state**, with an explicit flip between the two phases. The design is grounded in the real EasyEcom Product Master API (five functions: GetProductMaster, GetProductMastersCount, CreateMasterProduct, UpdateMasterProduct, ActivateDeactivateProduct) and real `GetProductMaster` payloads.

### 8.1.1 Two phases, one explicit flip

- **Onboarding (bidirectional, supervised, one-time per client):** products may already exist on either side. The integration pulls EasyEcom products into ERPNext **and** pushes ERPNext items into EasyEcom, reconciling both catalogues into a single mapped set. This is a supervised migration, not a steady state — duplicates and conflicts are surfaced for FDE resolution.
- **Steady state (ERPNext-mastered):** once the FDE marks onboarding complete, **ERPNext is the source of truth for items.** New items and changes flow ERPNext → EasyEcom (push). EasyEcom is downstream for the item master.
- **The flip is explicit:** a mode flag (`item_master_mode`: `onboarding` | `erpnext_mastered`) on the EasyEcom Account / per-client settings. The FDE flips it only after reconciliation is complete. Behaviour differs by phase (below).

### 8.1.2 The EasyEcom Item Map (direction-agnostic correspondence registry)

The link between an EasyEcom product and an ERPNext item/bundle is an explicit, persistent record — **not** a field-equality computation. This is the single source of linkage for both directions and all downstream flows (orders, inventory).

- `EasyEcom Item Map` — one row per correspondence:
  - `ee_sku` (the EasyEcom SKU — EE's unique product identifier per its own FAQ; the join key)
  - `erpnext_item` (the linked ERPNext Item **or** Product Bundle — the link accommodates both object types, via a Dynamic Link or a type field)
  - `ee_product_id`, `ee_cp_id` (EasyEcom internal ids — stored because the push functions, Update/ActivateDeactivate, key on `product_id`; not our join key)
  - status / flags (Mapped, Created-Flagged, Flagged-Not-Created, Drift, Disabled)
- The map is **direction-agnostic**: a row is created whether the product was born in EasyEcom (pull created it) or in ERPNext (push created it in EE). It is also the **component-identity resolver** for combos/bundles in both directions (below).

### 8.1.3 Matching (deliberately simple)

EasyEcom guarantees SKU uniqueness (its FAQ: "SKU is the most unique identifier"; "sellers are mandated to maintain unique SKU codes"). So:

- **Map row already exists** → use it.
- **EE `sku` exactly equals an ERPNext `item_code`** (byte-for-byte, case- and whitespace-sensitive) → **auto-map** to that item + create the map row.
- **Anything else** → **create a new ERPNext item + a new map row.**

There is no fuzzy matching, no EAN-based matching, and no matching-review queue. The chosen error direction is "never wrongly link" over "never duplicate": a non-exact case creates a new item (a visible, fixable duplicate at worst) rather than risk an incorrect auto-link (silent data corruption). `item_code` defaults to `sku` for items created by the pull.

### 8.1.4 EE → ERPNext (pull)

The pull is the **inbound onboarding** flow, and after the flip becomes **drift detection** (below).

- **Endpoint:** `GET /Products/GetProductMaster` (headers `x-api-key` + per-account `Bearer <jwt>`). `includeLocations=1` → **account-wide catalogue** (products are a catalogue keyed by globally-unique SKU; not a per-location sweep). `GET /Products/GetProductMastersCount` gives the total for progress.
- **Job shape:** the catalogue is large (counts in the tens of thousands). The pull is a **cursor-paginated, count-aware, resumable background job**: read the count, then walk the `nextUrl` cursor page by page (≤200/page), **savepoint-isolated per product** (one bad product does not abort the page or the walk; the job resumes from the last cursor if interrupted). It is **not** a single synchronous call (which would time out).
- **Incremental:** full pull on onboarding; `updated_after` deltas on the daily schedule.
- **Field translation via the Field Mapping engine** (the `EasyEcom-Item-Pull` ruleset, reconciled to the real `GetProductMaster` payload — the shipped ruleset is stale, §8.0 policy). Maps `sku`, `product_name`, `description`, `brand`, `colour`, `size`, dimensions (with unit handling), `mrp`, `cost`, `hsn_code` → `gst_hsn_code`, `accounting_unit` → `stock_uom` (with a **default-UOM** transform for missing/garbage values — EE supplies no clean stock UOM), `active` → `disabled`, `custom_fields`.
- **Tax (stamped for ALL enabled Companies):** because an item is account-wide/shared but tax is per-Company, after each Item upsert the pull **loops the Account's enabled EasyEcom Company Settings and calls 8c's `resolve_and_stamp_tax(item, ee_product, company)` once per Company.** Each call stamps *that company's* Item Tax rows (its company-specific templates + Min/Max Net Rate bands). **All companies' rows coexist on the one shared item's Taxes table** (ERPNext resolves the correct row per transaction by matching the template's company natively — this is the intended ERPNext one-item-many-companies tax model). The product carries `tax_rule_name`, `tax_rate`, `cess`. The per-Company stamping must be **append (companies never clobber each other) and idempotent (a re-pull does not duplicate a company's rows)**. Unmapped (tax_rule_name, company) → 8c auto-creates a To-Configure Tax Rule Map + flags FDE for that company.
- **product_type branching** (on the **response string** value; the doc's integer enums are inconsistent across endpoints and are a push-side concern only):
  - `normal_product` → ERPNext **Item**
  - `combo_product` → ERPNext **Product Bundle** (its `sub_products` become bundle components, each resolved to an ERPNext item via its own map row)
  - `variant_parent` / `child_product` / `kit_bom` / **any unknown or future type (e.g. a future "digital" type)** → **flag, do NOT create.** Variants, variant children, and kits/BOMs should not exist in an EasyEcom↔ERPNext item integration (manufacturing/variant modelling stays ERPNext-side); their appearance in the EE pull is an exception for the FDE, not a creation target. Unknown types fall through to the same flag (forward-safe).
- **Content gating** splits by whether the problem blocks ERPNext Item creation:
  - **Missing HSN → flag-not-created (held).** India Compliance enforces `gst_hsn_code` as mandatory on Item, so an item with no HSN **cannot be created** — it is **flagged (Flagged-Not-Created), not created** (we do not supply a placeholder HSN, which would risk a wrong HSN flowing into a real transaction, and we do not override India Compliance's validation). This is the one content problem that holds rather than creates.
  - **Unmapped tax rule or dirty/missing UOM → create + flag (Created-Flagged).** These do **not** block Item creation, so the item is **created and flagged** (the SKU exists for downstream orders) and surfaced in the review queue — never silently defaulted.
  - Nothing is ever silently defaulted; the only thing that holds is missing HSN (because the platform makes it un-createable).
- **`is_stock_item` is set per product nature, not hardcoded.** Normal products are stock items (`is_stock_item=1`); Product Bundle wrapper items are non-stock (`is_stock_item=0`, §8.1.6); a future "digital" product type will also be non-stock (`is_stock_item=0`). The item-creation path takes `is_stock_item` as an input (default 1) so bundles and future non-physical types set it without rework.
- `active: 0` in the payload → the ERPNext item is **disabled** (never deleted).

### 8.1.5 ERPNext → EE (push)

The push is the **outbound onboarding** flow **and** the **steady-state mechanism** (once ERPNext is master, all item flow is push).

- **Endpoints:** `POST /Products/CreateMasterProduct` (new), `POST /Products/UpdateMasterProduct` (changed; keys on sku or productId; supports partial updates), `POST /Products/ActivateDeactivateProduct` (lifecycle; keys on product_id).
- **Field translation** ERPNext item → EE create-payload. EasyEcom's create requires fields ERPNext items do not natively carry — these are **manufactured/defaulted** on push: `materialType` (mandatory in EE; absent in ERPNext → default **Finished Good = 1**), `itemType` (from the product's nature: normal = 0, combo = 1), `Brand` / `Category` / `ModelNumber` (sourced from the item or defaulted). `ProductTaxCode` = the item's HSN. `TaxRate` from the item's tax (allowed EE values 0/3/5/12/18/28). Dimensions in EE units (weight g, dimensions cm).
- On `CreateMasterProduct`, EE returns `product_id` → written to the map row.

### 8.1.6 Product Bundles ↔ EasyEcom combos (bidirectional)

Bundles are bidirectional, mirroring normal items:

- **Pull:** EE `combo_product` → ERPNext Product Bundle (covered in 8.1.4).
- **Push:** an ERPNext **Product Bundle** → an EasyEcom **combo** (a "virtual combo" — non-stocked selling wrapper; the components carry the stock, exactly as a Product Bundle is non-stocked in ERPNext). Pushed via `CreateMasterProduct` with `itemType = 1` (Combo) and the `subProducts` array assembled from the bundle's components — **each component resolved to its EasyEcom SKU via its own map row.**
- **Dependency ordering:** components must exist in EasyEcom (and be mapped) before the combo is pushed. The push processes **components before combos**; if a component is missing or unmapped at combo-push time, the combo is **flagged, not pushed** (no broken combos).
- **Constraint:** EasyEcom requires a combo's `subProducts` to contain **at least 2 products**; a bundle with fewer cannot be pushed and is flagged.
- **The bundle gets its own map row** (bundle ↔ EE combo); its components resolve through their own rows. The map is thus the **component-identity resolver in both directions** (sub_product sku → ERPNext item on pull; ERPNext component → EE sku on push).
- **No BOM / no Kit:** ERPNext BOMs are **not** pushed to EasyEcom (manufacturing stays ERPNext-side); EasyEcom `kit_bom` is flagged-not-created on pull. Only Product Bundles ↔ combos cross the boundary.

### 8.1.7 Lifecycle — deactivate / reactivate (both directions, phase-governed)

- **Onboarding:** EE `active` flag → ERPNext disable/enable (pull side); ERPNext disable → EE `ActivateDeactivateProduct(status=0)` (push side).
- **Steady state (ERPNext-mastered):** ERPNext disable/enable → pushed to EE. An EE-side deactivation is **not** accepted as truth — it is **flagged as drift** (below).
- Deactivation never deletes on either side.

### 8.1.8 After the flip — drift detection

Once `item_master_mode = erpnext_mastered`:

- The push (ERPNext → EE) is the authoritative item flow.
- The pull continues only as **drift detection**: an EE-originated new product, or an EE-side edit to a mapped item, is **flagged for the FDE** — not accepted as truth, and **not auto-overwritten** from ERPNext (silently overwriting could destroy legitimate EE operational data; silently accepting would violate ERPNext mastership). The FDE decides per drift flag.

### 8.1.9 Onboarding reconciliation (supervised) and the exception/review state

- Onboarding reconciliation is **FDE-supervised**: after pulling EE products and pushing ERPNext items, duplicates (same physical product reached from both sides with non-matching identifiers) and conflicts are surfaced; the FDE resolves them; then the FDE flips to `erpnext_mastered`.
- The **review/exception state** holds three kinds of items, all visible to the FDE:
  - **Created-Flagged** — created but with a content problem that does not block creation (unmapped tax, dirty/missing UOM) to fix.
  - **Flagged-Not-Created** — not created: either an unsupported type (variant_parent / child_product / kit_bom / unknown) **or** a product missing HSN (India Compliance makes an HSN-less Item un-createable, §8.1.4).
  - **Drift** — (post-flip) an unexpected EE-side change or EE-origin product.

### 8.1.10 Build sequence (one packet, internally sequential)

Built as a single packet, in dependency order: (1) the `EasyEcom Item Map` + the `item_master_mode` substrate; (2) the EE → ERPNext pull (cursor job, engine ruleset reconciliation, tax-resolver integration, product_type branching, content gating); (3) the ERPNext → EE push (create/update, EE-mandatory-field manufacturing); (4) Product Bundle ↔ combo both ways (component resolution, dependency ordering); (5) lifecycle (deactivate/reactivate, phase-governed) and the post-flip drift detection. Reuses the Field Mapping engine (§8.0), 8c's tax resolver, the 8a per-record savepoint helper, and the workflow-fixture pattern (and its captured gotchas, including the `safe_eval`/`len()` condition rule).

### 8.1.11 Out of scope (separate, later)

- **Marketplace listings** (Amazon ASIN, Flipkart FSN, per-channel `marketplace_sku`): synced per-channel from the marketplaces into EasyEcom (EE FAQ); a separate listing-sync concern, not part of the item master.
- **Stock / inventory sync** (per-location quantities): a separate concern from the product master (which is the account-wide catalogue).
- Account-global sharing across Companies via ERPNext Item Defaults is the standard ERPNext mechanism; the integration does not duplicate the Item per Company.
